theory.intro: On a number line, values increase from left to right. Use that to compare and order integers.
facts: Any negative is less than any positive; $0$ sits between them.
steps: Picture the integers on a number line.
The one further left is smaller.

foundation.q: Complete: $-4$ is ____ than $0$. Then write with $<$ or $>$.

development.q: Order these temperatures from coldest to warmest: $3, -7, -1, 5, -4$ (°C).

mastery.q: List all integers greater than $-65$ and less than $-58$.
Which is at the lowest altitude: Caspian Sea ($-28$ m), Dead Sea ($-408$ m), Lake Eyre ($-15$ m)?
List all integers greater than $-34$ and less than $-28$.
Warsaw is warmer than Moscow ($-9$°C) but colder than Toronto ($-3$°C). Give a possible temperature for Warsaw.
A student wrote $-6 > -2$. Is this correct? Explain.
